Agents safely emitting domain events without violating invariants, avoiding runaway multi-agent feedback loops, plus audit/explainability hits Sean's provenance-substrate and trust-boundary signals.

technical.trackName: AI app platform & prod LLM ops
technical.rationale: Continuous context-based code-review/governance with proof-of-validation reports is conceptually on-topic but this is a Qodo CEO vision talk thin on concrete architecture.

Production Neo4j knowledge graph modeled for agentic consumption behind one MCP server, framed as the durable moat over commoditized model/framework layers, hits graph+agents and model-as-moat dead-on.

200M-interaction production post-mortem where benchmarks-arent-enough, interaction signals become training data, and a 30+ model supervisor constellation catches failures one LLM misses, squarely Track A and B with real numbers.

technical.trackName: AI app platform & prod LLM ops
technical.rationale: CoreWeave multi-tier inference platform spanning pay-per-token to self-managed is real ops lessons but centers on serving infrastructure rather than agent orchestration or eval.
Browser/search/scraper primitives for agents leaving the sandbox is a thin vendor demo with little on Sean's eval, memory, or trust-boundary signals.
Agents that generate/test/deploy their own tools at runtime with explicit safety boundaries hits harness design and capability-scoping, though the AWS-advocate framing leans demo over post-mortem.
Directly targets harness architecture and production failure modes (context rot, premature completion, tool bloat) that map exactly to Sean's orchestration/harness track.
MCP/CLI/Skills tooling-layer choices touch Sean's stack but the abstract stays at overview altitude with no production tradeoffs or numbers.
Graph-based execution-DAG anomaly detection with concrete production numbers (41x node deviation, 2-3hr to 30s) fits the graph+observability stretch lane but it is API middleware, not agents.
AI couples-therapy product and regulatory framing is off Sean's agent-infrastructure focus with no transferable systems lesson.
Inference-silicon hardware angle sits below Sean's application/architecture layer and the one-line abstract gives nothing concrete.
Pure vision keynote lining up ChatGPT/Claude Code/OpenClaw to guess what is next, exactly the hype-with-no-substance Sean's negative list rules out.
Per-session microVM isolation with a live escape attempt and host boundary is squarely Sean's agent-security/blast-radius track with concrete demonstration.

Privacy-preserving patterns (revocable handles, hashed identifiers, agent boundaries, confidential computing) hit the trust-boundary/leakage-via-surrounding-systems theme though it leans DevRel-practical over post-mortem.
Open-source agent harness with persistent sandboxed workspaces, compounding memory, and typed Python generated from MCP schemas matches harness + memory + MCP signals directly.
Built-and-used war-story on team coordination, shared agent sessions, and cloud dev environments addresses multi-agent orchestration bottlenecks and review loops.

technical.trackName: Agent memory & generative UI / agent-native UX
technical.rationale: Shipped video memory layer framed as a context-graph systems concept with entity resolution and ground-every-claim-to-source provenance fits agent-memory-beyond-RAG plus provenance substrate signals.

Using X12 as a deterministic, validation-and-acknowledgment harness for stochastic agents is a near-perfect match to enforcing correctness structurally with auditability and human review in a messy domain.
Transformer-only inference ASIC is below Sean's stack with a one-line abstract offering no architectural takeaway for him.

technical.trackName: Agent memory & generative UI / agent-native UX
technical.rationale: Reframing context graphs as control planes at scale (sub-300ms, data-freshness tiering, mandatory provenance, fetch-less decisions) is exactly the context-system architecture and provenance thinking Sean wants.

technical.trackName: Agent memory & generative UI / agent-native UX
technical.rationale: Interface composed per-intent by an orchestrator running in production for enterprise commerce hits the generative-UI/agent-native-UX track and strongly appeals to Sean's design-engineering side, though it carries vendor framing.

Production multi-agent LangGraph system over AP/ERP with RBAC and three named failure modes that kill finance pilots delivers the orchestration war-story and dev-loop-equals-prod-primitives lesson Sean values.

Hits his core theme of judging non-deterministic agents in production incidents and how to evaluate when an investigation was right, but the TBD speaker and open-research-agenda framing keep it from being a concrete war-story.

technical.trackName: Agent memory & generative UI / agent-native UX
technical.rationale: Real on-device multi-tool agent architecture with persistent per-user memory and intervention decisions is genuinely interesting agent-native UX, but it is Kotlin/Koog on a local quantized model, off his TS/Python LangGraph stack.
A concrete failure-mode war-story on document parsing across hard corpora that is adjacent to his data-pipeline interest, but parsing is not a core agent-eval, orchestration, or feedback-loop signal.

technical.trackName: Agent memory & generative UI / agent-native UX
technical.rationale: Durable human-in-the-loop agents with approval gates, timeout/escalation, graceful halt and resume on LangGraph plus Temporal directly hits his human-feedback-as-endpoint and reversibility signals with two production-ready designs.

Maps the harness surface (orchestration, governance, memory core, continual-learning loops) which is his exact territory, but the Oracle-primitive framing and buzzword density dilute the transferable substance.

A stakes x evidence matrix for agent authorization spanning OAuth scopes, Claude Code allow/ask/deny, and cryptographically-bound payment mandates is real capability-scoping and provable-audit architecture that generalizes past payments.
GitHub Next vision piece on realtime-multiplayer coding and human-context capture is thought-provoking but light on architecture, numbers, or post-mortem substance.
Concrete prompting and multi-pass pipeline decisions for event-driven doc generation, but auto-documentation is tangential to his eval, orchestration, and feedback-loop focus.
A what-to-build product-judgment thesis serves his lighter AI-product-strategy goal, but it carries no technical depth and is squarely off his strong-match signals.
Harness engineering as making the codebase agent-reliable is on-theme, but the short abstract reads as a Tessl product pitch with shallow how-to framing.

An eval flywheel that captures user-perspective success from production workflows and feeds back into product bets is almost exactly his highest-priority Track A goal of sharpening eval and feedback loops.

A field guide to a multi-agent SDLC factory sharing context across stages touches his orchestration-at-scale interest, but the abstract leans toward an operating-model pitch over hard architecture detail.

A multi-agent creative computer orchestrating five generative models across text, image, video, and audio is a strong creative/generative-media wildcard hit even though the technical depth is demo-level.

technical.trackName: AI app platform & prod LLM ops
technical.rationale: A real org war-story about parallel agent work breaking team coherence and invisible decisions made in agent chats is insightful but org/process rather than his core infra signals.

Surrounding agents with enforceable ontology constraints (typed entities, cardinality, shared vocabulary that catches malformed tool calls) is exactly his structural-correctness-over-prompts signal plus graph+agents.

A coarse-to-fine synthetic generation pipeline with human-in-the-loop steering to build trustworthy golden eval datasets under PHI constraints directly feeds his eval/feedback-loop focus with a concrete production process.

Supervised promotion turns human knowledge-sharing calls into the training signal for orchestrator agents, hitting feedback-loop, handoffs, and memory-beyond-RAG simultaneously with a what-the-first-build-got-wrong post-mortem.
Real lock-in / connective-layer framing matches build-vs-buy, but the back half is a three-alpha Aperture product pitch with little transferable post-mortem substance.
Tutorial-framed hard/simple/zero-code evolution narrative that is near getting-started territory and offers little novel to someone already fluent in MCP, Deep Agents, and skills.
Claude plus Three.js as a game-dev partner is off his agent-infra layer technically but lands squarely on his maker/creative-tooling side for high creativeAppeal.
Names the lethal-trifecta trust-boundary problem but centers on a vague little-known-law reveal and registry vision rather than concrete architectural patterns.

A year of production multi-tenant personal agent with named stack covers permissioning, message-time guardrails, prompt-injection defense, and FAISS+Neo4j durable memory, a concrete defensive-engineering war story.
An honest production post-mortem on why they killed a multi-agent pipeline, with derive-don't-impose architecture, knowledge-graph context, and deterministic complements, despite the sponsor tag.
Anthropic/Anterior eval methodology for translating real-world performance into measurement rubrics with imperfect synthetic data is dead-center Track A and goal #1.

technical.trackName: AI app platform & prod LLM ops
technical.rationale: The multiplicative-cost framing for agentic inference is a real architecture concern, but the talk is a founder-CEO vendor pitch thin on concrete optimization specifics.
Gateway-as-runtime governance with per-agent identity, short-lived scoped credentials, PII stripping, and what-breaks-when-removed is straight Track C, but no speaker is listed.

socials.rationale: Opening reception with 100+ partners is the highest-serendipity room of the week for scouting agent-infra players and seeding repositioning conversations.
Invite-only and high-signal but Oxylabs is proxy/scraping infra tangential to his agent-systems focus, and access is uncertain.
Qodo's AI-coding-agent crowd overlaps his agent-infra and harness interests, and the VIP format favors quality peer connections.
Speakers-only private dinner is not accessible to him as a non-speaker.
Community meetups self-select for deep-dive practitioners, ideal for finding leading thinkers and peers in his repositioning space.
The main onsite networking night gathers the full attendee base, the densest low-effort opportunity to meet agent-infra peers.
An invite-only sports suite is socially pleasant but a diluted, low-targeting setting for the technical peer connections he wants.
Startup night with founders/billing crowd lightly serves his AI-product-strategy goal but is off-center from agent-systems infra.
Same strong meetup dynamic on Day 3, topic-focused rooms good for peers and scouting what's next in agent infra.
Vercel's crowd sits squarely on his Next/AI-SDK/AG-UI stack and DevRel/OSS goal, a prime room for relevant peer and thought-leader connections.
Closing-night meetups stay topic-focused and worthwhile, though attendance and energy typically thin out by the final evening.
